pattern: npm install express-oauth2-jwt-bearer|yarn add express-oauth2-jwt-bearer|pnpm add express-oauth2-jwt-bearer|"express-oauth2-jwt-bearer"
description: Auth0 SDK installed via any valid method
Has correct SDK package name in source
Configures issuer base URL for JWT validation
Configures API audience for token validation
Has protected API endpoint with auth middleware
Auth0 domain written to correct config file (.env)
API audience written to correct config file (.env)
No login UI methods in stateless API
No client secret in API config (uses JWKS)

question: Does the solution correctly configure Auth0 JWT Bearer token validation, protected endpoints, and CORS in an Express API using express-oauth2-jwt-bearer? The correctness criteria are: (1) auth() middleware with issuerBaseURL and audience, (2) at least one protected route, (3) CORS configured before auth, (4) public and private endpoint separation.
examples:
PASS: Validates JWT using JWKS endpoint via auth(), checks audience, has public + protected routes, CORS before auth.
FAIL: Uses client secret for token validation instead of JWKS.
FAIL: Has login routes — APIs should be stateless JWT only.
FAIL: Uses express-jwt or jsonwebtoken instead of express-oauth2-jwt-bearer.
description: Correctness: solution correctly integrates Auth0 JWT validation into Express API
Uses current SDK version (1.7.x) in dependency declaration
Accesses permissions claim for RBAC authorization
Uses dotenv for environment variable management
Does not use wrong SDK (SPA/mobile SDK in API project)

question: Check ONLY for these skill-specific advanced patterns (NOT basic SDK usage like auth, cors, express). This is a skill attribution check. Look for at least 2 of: (1) SDK version 1.7 in a dependency declaration, (2) requiredScopes or scopeIncludesAny for scope-based authorization, (3) req.auth.payload.permissions for RBAC permission checking, (4) claimEquals or claimIncludes or claimCheck for custom claim validation, (5) UnauthorizedError or InvalidTokenError or InsufficientScopeError for typed error handling. These are advanced patterns taught by the skill that are NOT part of standard quickstart knowledge. Answer YES only if at least 2 of these advanced patterns are present, NO otherwise.
examples:
PASS: Uses requiredScopes('read:messages') AND accesses req.auth.payload.permissions for RBAC.
PASS: Uses version 1.7.x AND has claimCheck for custom validation.
FAIL: Only uses basic auth() middleware with no advanced configuration.
description: Skill attribution: uses advanced skill-specific patterns beyond basic SDK usage
